title: rosa glauca subsp. caballicensis
intro: Rosa glauca subsp. caballicensis, also known as Caballero's rose or Rosa glauca subsp. caballicensis, is a subspecies of the Rosa glauca, a species of rose native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa glauca subsp. caballicensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to gastrointestinal problems.
